auth_module_map.role: Defines AbstractBaseUser (lines 43-164) with password and last_login fields; BaseUserManager (lines 21-40) provides user creation utilities
Defines AbstractUser (lines 446-514) extending AbstractBaseUser + PermissionsMixin; defines PermissionsMixin (lines 317-443) with groups and user_permissions M2M fields
Defines User concrete model (lines 517-526) as default Django user; UserManager (lines 140-239) handles user creation with username validation
Initial migration defining User model schema with all fields
Defines UnicodeUsernameValidator used in AbstractUser.username field

migration_trace: 1. Start with AbstractBaseUser (base_user.py:43-164): password (CharField, line 44), last_login (DateTimeField, line 45)

2. AbstractUser extends AbstractBaseUser + PermissionsMixin (models.py:446); adds username (line 454-457), first_name (line 468), last_name (line 469), email (line 470), is_staff (line 471), is_active (line 476), date_joined (line 484)
3. PermissionsMixin (models.py:317-443) adds groups M2M (line 331) and user_permissions M2M (line 342)
4. User concrete model (models.py:517-526) uses UserManager (models.py:140-239) for creation
5. Initial migration (migrations/0001_initial.py) captures all fields in database schema
6. Custom app must add 3 fields not in AbstractUser/AbstractBaseUser: profile_tier, external_sso_id, last_sync_at

unmappable_fields.reason: No CharField with choices equivalent exists in AbstractUser or AbstractBaseUser. AbstractUser provides basic user attributes (username, email, first_name, last_name) and auth state (is_staff, is_active, date_joined) but no application-specific tier/subscription fields.
unmappable_fields.migration_strategy: Add profile_tier as custom field on custom User model extending AbstractBaseUser: `profile_tier = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[('free', 'Free'), ('pro', 'Pro'), ('enterprise', 'Enterprise')], default='free')`. This field will coexist with Django auth fields without conflict.
unmappable_fields.evidence: AbstractUser field definitions (models.py:454-484) show only standard auth fields; no field with choices pattern exists for subscription/tier management

unmappable_fields.reason: AbstractBaseUser and AbstractUser have no unique nullable CharField for external system identifiers. AbstractBaseUser only tracks password/last_login; AbstractUser adds username (which has unique constraint by default) but it's reserved for Django auth. External SSO IDs require separate tracking.
unmappable_fields.migration_strategy: Add external_sso_id as custom field on custom User model: `external_sso_id = models.CharField(max_length=255, unique=True, null=True, blank=True)`. Document that this field is managed separately from AbstractUser.username to enable SSO-based login via authentication backend.
unmappable_fields.evidence: AbstractUser defines username field (models.py:454-457) with UnicodeUsernameValidator (validators.py reference); no SSO identifier field. UserManager._create_user_object (models.py:143-156) normalizes username but has no SSO field handling.

unmappable_fields.reason: AbstractBaseUser provides last_login (base_user.py:45); AbstractUser adds date_joined (models.py:484). Neither tracks external system sync timestamps. last_login records auth events; date_joined records account creation; neither fits an external sync timestamp.
unmappable_fields.migration_strategy: Add last_sync_at as custom field on custom User model: `last_sync_at = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)`. This field will be updated independently via custom signals or management commands when syncing with external SSO provider; it does not conflict with Django's auth_user.last_login.
unmappable_fields.evidence: base_user.py:45 defines last_login as DateTimeField; models.py:484 defines date_joined. Neither serves sync tracking purpose. update_last_login signal (models.py:18-24) updates only last_login on authentication, not sync time.
